frameworks.name: Bayesian hierarchy discovery
frameworks.one_line: formal Bayesian model infers state clusters supporting hierarchical planning

frameworks.name: hierarchical planning
frameworks.one_line: decomposing tasks into sub-problems via discovered state abstractions

frameworks.name: structure learning
frameworks.one_line: principles imported to motivate discovery of useful abstractions

frameworks.name: robotics hierarchical decomposition
frameworks.one_line: robotics-derived hierarchy principles applied to human planning

core_question: How do humans spontaneously discover hierarchical abstractions of environments to enable efficient planning?
key_assumptions: humans cluster environment states into useful abstractions
hierarchy discovery is sensitive to topology, reward, and task distribution
hierarchical representations support breaking problems into sub-problems
manipulations: distribution of tasks within the environment
key_findings: model accounts for prior effects of environment structure on planning behavior
model captures detection of bottleneck states and transitions
task and reward distributions influence planning via discovered hierarchy
hierarchy discovery unfolds incrementally across trials
interpretations: humans spontaneously organize environments into clusters supporting hierarchical planning
useful abstractions emerge from a Bayesian inference process over environment statistics
